scenarios.name: single-feature-local-no-pm
scenarios.query: Build a local wiki of the features I built in this repo.
scenarios.setup: Small repo, commits by one author, conventional-commit scopes, NO project-management MCP connected.
expected_behavior: Resolves the author from git config and scopes the mine to that author
Runs mine_history.py and groups commits into features
Falls back to link-only mode (no ticket enrichment) without erroring
Writes one doc per feature with the dedup marker on line 1 and no markdown tables
Renders a local index.html + content.js that opens without a server

scenarios.name: jira-confluence-rerun-dedup
scenarios.query: Publish my feature docs to Confluence.
scenarios.setup: Repo with PROJ-* commits; Jira + Confluence MCPs connected; a prior run already published two of the features (recorded in manifest AND present on Confluence).
expected_behavior: Detects and binds Jira (read) and Confluence (write) by capability, not hardcoded names
Enriches features with Jira descriptions and links
For already-published features, SEARCHES Confluence by the featureWikiId marker and UPDATES in place
Creates pages only for genuinely new features
Never creates a second page for a ticket that already has one
Records every published page in the manifest

scenarios.name: shared-ticket-multiple-authors
scenarios.query: Document the features I shipped, scope to me.
scenarios.setup: A ticket/epic touched by several authors; a colleague already created the page for that ticket on the target.
expected_behavior: Scopes commit mining to the requesting author only
Still resolves the feature identity to the shared ticket/epic key
Finds the colleague's existing page via marker search and updates it rather than duplicating
Flags any pre-existing duplicate pages for manual merge instead of auto-deleting
